rationale: Thomas (2001) is cited not just in a background list but to support a core premise: acquisition conditions affect later relationship outcomes. The citing paper also uses it in the modeling section as prior work that jointly links acquisition and retention and explicitly notes its own linkage and censoring treatment in that context. Because this prior study helps motivate both the theory and the joint-model setup, the citation plays a meaningful developmental role.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Thomas (2001)'s idea that acquisition and retention are linked by modeling reacquisition and second-tenure duration jointly in a winback setting. It extends that line of work by adding pricing effects and restart-customer targeting and SLTV implications within a split-hazard framework.
